channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kakamega Police Ban Reggae na Lami Concerts Due to Traffic and Public Safety Concerns
item.description: On Wednesday, May 6, 2026, Kakamega authorities officially banned the popular 'Reggae na Lami' funeral ritual following multiple complaints from motorists and local traders. The ritual, deeply rooted ...

item.title: Health officials urge vigilance after hepatitis A cases in Barry
item.description: Health authorities in Barry, Vale of Glamorgan, are advising parents to ensure children maintain thorough hand hygiene following a small outbreak of hepatitis A. The infection has been identified in t...

item.title: Coalition explores reducing annual net overseas migration to 150,000-200,000
item.description: The Coalition is considering a significant reduction in Australia's net overseas migration, with internal documents revealing plans to lower the annual figure to between 150,000 and 200,000. This repr...

item.title: Yanga SC ends contract with coach Pedro Gonçalves after seven months
item.description: Young Africans SC, better known as Yanga, have officially ended their working relationship with Portuguese coach Pedro Gonçalves after a seven-month tenure. Gonçalves joined the Tanzanian football gia...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee refuses to resign after BJP majority in West Bengal, alleges electoral irregularities
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has declined to resign despite her party, the Trinamool Congress (TMC), suffering a decisive defeat in the 2026 Assembly elections, where the Bharatiya Janat...

item.title: Supreme Court Suspends Operations in Mandaue and Cebu City Courts During 48th ASEAN Summit
item.description: The Supreme Court of the Philippines has ordered the suspension of work at the first and second level courts in Mandaue City and Cebu City from noon of May 6 until May 8, 2026. This temporary halt is ...

item.title: Storm in Garden Route claims social worker’s life and closes schools
item.description: A severe storm has hit the Garden Route region, resulting in tragic consequences and widespread disruption. Lauren Fredericks, a social worker from the Knysna office, died when a tree fell on her car ...

item.title: New York Library Opens to Exhibit Complete Epstein Files and Honor Survivors
item.description: A new reading room in New York City's Tribeca neighborhood has opened, dedicated entirely to the Jeffrey Epstein files. The Donald J. Trump and Jeffrey Epstein Memorial Reading Room, organized by the ...

item.title: Study explores psychological impact of preterm birth on mothers in Ghana
item.description: Preterm birth, which is delivery before 37 weeks, affects about 10% of babies worldwide, with around 15 million born too early every year. Survival rates are strongly tied to a country's resources: in...

item.title: Singer Bonnie Tyler Undergoes Emergency Intestinal Surgery in Portugal
item.description: Renowned singer Bonnie Tyler, aged 74, has been admitted to hospital in Faro, Portugal, for urgent intestinal surgery. According to her spokesperson, the procedure was successful and she is currently ...

item.title: BCE terminates a few employees for repeatedly falsifying office attendance
item.description: BCE Inc., the parent company of Bell Canada, has terminated a small number of employees after discovering that they intentionally falsified their workplace attendance. The internal review revealed a p...

item.title: Geopolitical tensions wipe nearly R350bn off PIC assets amid market turmoil
item.description: The Public Investment Corporation (PIC), which manages funds including those of the Government Employee Pension Fund (GEPF), has reported a significant decline of প্রায় R350 billion in assets under ma...

item.title: Father Seeks Justice After Son Fatally Shot in Parow Valley
item.description: The Sampson family is grappling with grief following the tragic murder of their 24-year-old son, Egan Sampson, in Parow Valley earlier this year. Egan was shot multiple times in Denton Street, Ravensm...

item.title: RSU Law Professor Proposes Amendment to Land Use Act to Ease Land Ownership
item.description: Prof Grace Ogbonda Akolokwu, wey be Professor of Property and Human Rights Law for Rivers State University, don yan say dem suppose remove the consent provisions wey dey Sections 21, 22 and 28 of the ...

item.title: Federal budget unlikely to resolve underlying housing supply issues
item.description: The upcoming federal budget has sparked extensive debate and pre-release speculation, particularly around proposed changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax discount. These tax incentives ...

item.title: Four Highway Patrol Group officers investigated over alleged extortion of TNVS driver
item.description: Four police officers from the Philippine National Police’s Highway Patrol Group (HPG) are currently under investigation following an extortion complaint filed by a transport network vehicle service (T...

item.title: Traditional Costumes and Rituals on Italian Islands Captured in Photography
item.description: British photographer Alice Tomlinson spent two years documenting the traditional costumes worn by residents of the Venetian Lagoon, Sicily, and Sardinia during local celebrations. Her project, titled ...

item.title: Cantada acknowledges nerves affected her performance in UAAP Finals
item.description: During the UAAP Season 88 Finals, National University volleyball player Sam Cantada admitted that she felt overwhelmed by the pressure of the championship matches. Speaking to the media, Cantada share...

item.title: Labour faces criticism for including Net Zero targets in pothole funding scheme
item.description: Labour has been criticised for its new pothole funding scheme, with accusations that the party has sought to favour councils under its control by including Net Zero targets in the scoring system. The ...

item.title: Vita Buivid's Photographic Exploration of War Through Family Memories
item.description: Vita Buivid, an artist originally from Dnipro and currently based in Amsterdam, created the photo series 'How I Spend My Summer,' where she combines idyllic family photographs with images of military ...
